name: R55 full supplied-class embedding census
hypothesis: all induced fixed-core pullbacks over 328 supplied graphs and 328 complements can be exhaustively reconciled by two implementations
expected_signal: 656 canonical host receipts with exact mapping/vector stream agreement and an independently checkable compressed manifest
decision_value: decides whether complete supplied-class boundary blocks exist and yields a reusable exact corpus; failure localizes the first incompatible or infeasible host

efficiency_design.naive_cost: For 656 labelled 42-vertex hosts, enumerate all injections of the fixed 30-vertex induced core, then all tied residual-row permutations; the raw injection space is 42P30 per host and is infeasible.
opportunities_considered: Symmetry reduction and canonical augmentation: retain a fixed labelled core and canonical row-lex residual ordering; expand every tied-row orbit so quotient coverage remains exact.
Bitsets, batching, and incremental constraints: custom DFS uses adjacency bitsets, most-constrained branching, forward degree constraints, and a Hall-union prune.

Memoization, caching, learned clauses, and reusable proof prefixes: graph/core parsing is reused per segment; DFS memoization was rejected pending a proof that state keys preserve labelled-map multiplicity; learned clauses are reserved for residual SAT.
Decomposition and resumability: split the corpus into immutable per-host units, source and complement halves, with atomic checkpoints and independently replayable segments.
Compressed streamed artifacts: write one gzip artifact per host plus an fsync'd JSONL manifest containing hashes and exact stream digests.

Sound cheap prefilters and independent coverage: degree/adjacency feasibility and Hall-union filters are one-sided; NetworkX VF2 independently enumerates every embedding and exact vector streams must agree.
efficiency_design.chosen_reductions: Use custom bitset DFS as the fast enumerator, NetworkX VF2 as an implementation-independent enumerator, exact tied-row orbit expansion, host-level decomposition, gzip artifacts, and hash-chained manifest/checkpoint records.
efficiency_design.expected_throughput_gain: Pilot timings imply about 5.6 aggregate core-hours for all 656 host representations instead of an impossible 42P30 scan. The first tranche re-estimates remaining time from observed host throughput.
efficiency_design.soundness_basis: Both enumerators must agree on embedding counts, mapping stream SHA-256, occurrence counts, unique vector counts, vector stream SHA-256, and exact unique vectors. Corpus SHA-256 and 328+328 coverage are independently checked; every completed host artifact is hash-addressed.
efficiency_design.remains_uncompressed: All supplied hosts and complements remain in scope; all induced embeddings and all tied-row variants remain represented. The workflow does not quotient the supplied graph corpus by complement/isomorphism and does not yet run residual SAT.

progress.message: reconciled 14/656 hosts with exact stream agreement
